title: arenaria graminea var. grandiflora
intro: Arenaria graminea var. grandiflora, also known as sea sandwort, is a flowering plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant's health benefits also include its potential to lower blood sugar levels and act as an antioxidant, protecting against cell damage and oxidative stress. Arenaria graminea var. grandiflora can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and support immune function.
